mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate at the zero lower bound forces investors out of cash and into equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 10% signals severe demand destruction and persistent earnings downgrade risk that weighs on the S&P 500 over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of months when UNRATE exceeds 10%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25% and the unemployment rate is simultaneously above 10%, the combined policy response pressure compresses the equity risk premium and drives sharp positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 70% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and UNRATE is above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in real retail sales indicate collapsing consumer demand that drags corporate revenues lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month in demand-driven recessions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production from a deep contraction signals a restart of manufacturing supply chains and inventory rebuilding, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below one million units signal a freeze in residential construction that spills over into construction employment and materials demand, dragging S&P 500 returns lower over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1000.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in nonfarm payrolls and a month-over-month fall in the unemployment rate signal a labor market inflection that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises MoM and UNRATE falls MoM
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when both PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls month-over-month in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 25 basis points signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months following a 25bp Fed funds hike.
